page: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ESv3 family. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 262 GB volume, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the processing capabilities of this instance are generally constrained. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance metrics from stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark consistently fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the bottom 25%, though the Passmark memory score achieves an average rating in the middle 50%. Computational tasks such as gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and image processing demonstrate weak to poor performance, reflecting the older processor architecture and limited core count.

The primary tradeoff of the E16-4s_v3 is its high memory capacity relative to its limited processing power. While the 128.0 GB of RAM provides substantial capacity for memory-bound applications, the weak CPU performance limits its efficiency for compute-intensive workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance is structured for workloads that require a large memory footprint but do not demand high CPU throughput, allowing users to avoid paying for unneeded vCPUs. Suitable workloads include memory-intensive database caching, lightweight database operations, and applications requiring large memory buffers with low CPU utilization.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v3 is a memory-optimized server instance from the ESv3 family, featuring an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The instance includes 262 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU resources. Performance benchmarks, including stress-ng and Geekbench, place its CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities mostly in the bottom 25%, though its Passmark memory score reaches the middle 50%. This profile presents a clear tradeoff: abundant memory capacity alongside constrained processing power. Consequently, the E16-4s_v3 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands, such as caching layers, small database operations, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v3 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, designed for memory-heavy workloads that require low CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v3 is a memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 262 GB storage, suited for low-CPU, memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server offering high RAM-to-vCPU density for memory-bound workloads with moderate processing requirements.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores
128.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB per core
262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average memory performance contrasted by lower-tier CPU benchmarks
Designed for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands
